text: Anno tertio regni Joakim regis Juda, venit Nabuchodonosor, rex Babylonis, in Jerusalem, et obsedit eam :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et tradidit Dominus in manu ejus Joakim, regem Juda, et partem vasorum domus Dei : et asportavit ea in terram Sennaar in domum dei sui, et vasa intulit in domum thesauri dei sui.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ait rex Asphenez præposito eunuchorum ut introduceret de filiis Israël, et de semine regio et tyrannorum,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: pueros in quibus nulla esset macula, decoros forma, et eruditos omni sapientia, cautos scientia, et doctos disciplina, et qui possent stare in palatio regis, ut doceret eos litteras et linguam Chaldæorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et constituit eis rex annonam per singulos dies de cibis suis, et de vino unde bibebat ipse, ut enutriti tribus annis, postea starent in conspectu regis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fuerunt ergo inter eos de filiis Juda, Daniel, Ananias, Misaël, et Azarias.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et imposuit eis præpositus eunuchorum nomina : Danieli, Baltassar ; Ananiæ, Sidrach ; Misaëli, Misach ; et Azariæ, Abdenago.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Proposuit autem Daniel in corde suo ne pollueretur de mensa regis, neque de vino potus ejus : et rogavit eunuchorum præpositum ne contaminaretur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dedit autem Deus Danieli gratiam et misericordiam in conspectu principis eunuchorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ait princeps eunuchorum ad Danielem : Timeo ego dominum meum regem, qui constituit vobis cibum et potum : qui si viderit vultus vestros macilentiores præ ceteris adolescentibus coævis vestris, condemnabitis caput meum regi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixit Daniel ad Malasar, quem constituerat princeps eunuchorum super Danielem, Ananiam, Misaëlem, et Azariam :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tenta nos, obsecro, servos tuos, diebus decem, et dentur nobis legumina ad vescendum, et aqua ad bibendum :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et contemplare vultus nostros, et vultus puerorum, qui vescuntur cibo regio : et sicut videris, facies cum servis tuis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Qui, audito sermone hujuscemodi, tentavit eos diebus decem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post dies autem decem, apparuerunt vultus eorum meliores, et corpulentiores præ omnibus pueris, qui vescebantur cibo regio.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro Malasar tollebat cibaria, et vinum potus eorum : dabatque eis legumina.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pueris autem his dedit Deus scientiam et disciplinam, in omni libro et sapientia : Danieli autem intelligentiam omnium visionum et somniorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Completis itaque diebus, post quos dixerat rex ut introducerentur, introduxit eos præpositus eunuchorum in conspectu Nabuchodonosor.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque eis locutus fuisset rex, non sunt inventi tales de universis, ut Daniel, Ananias, Misaël, et Azarias : et steterunt in conspectu regis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et omne verbum sapientiæ et intellectus, quod sciscitatus est ab eis rex, invenit in eis decuplum super cunctos ariolos et magos qui erant in universo regno ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fuit autem Daniel usque ad annum primum Cyri regis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In anno secundo regni Nabuchodonosor, vidit Nabuchodonosor somnium, et conterritus est spiritus ejus, et somnium ejus fugit ab eo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Præcepit autem rex ut convocarentur arioli, et magi, et malefici, et Chaldæi, ut indicarent regi somnia sua. Qui cum venissent, steterunt coram rege.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixit ad eos rex : Vidi somnium, et mente confusus ignoro quid viderim.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Responderuntque Chaldæi regi syriace : Rex, in sempiternum vive ! dic somnium servis tuis, et interpretationem ejus indicabimus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et respondens rex ait Chaldæis : Sermo recessit a me : nisi indicaveritis mihi somnium, et conjecturam ejus, peribitis vos, et domus vestræ publicabuntur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si autem somnium, et conjecturam ejus narraveritis, præmia, et dona, et honorem multum accipietis a me. Somnium igitur, et interpretationem ejus indicate mihi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Responderunt secundo, atque dixerunt : Rex somnium dicat servis suis, et interpretationem illius indicabimus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondit rex, et ait : Certe novi quod tempus redimitis, scientes quod recesserit a me sermo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si ergo somnium non indicaveritis mihi, una est de vobis sententia, quod interpretationem quoque fallacem, et deceptione plenam composueritis, ut loquamini mihi donec tempus pertranseat. Somnium itaque dicite mihi, ut sciam quod interpretationem quoque ejus veram loquamini.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondentes ergo Chaldæi coram rege, dixerunt : Non est homo super terram, qui sermonem tuum, rex, possit implere : sed neque regum quisquam magnus et potens verbum hujuscemodi sciscitatur ab omni ariolo, et mago, et Chaldæo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sermo enim, quem tu quæris, rex, gravis est : nec reperietur quisquam qui indicet illum in conspectu regis, exceptis diis, quorum non est cum hominibus conversatio.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quo audito, rex, in furore et in ira magna, præcepit ut perirent omnes sapientes Babylonis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et egressa sententia, sapientes interficiebantur : quærebanturque Daniel et socii ejus, ut perirent.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc Daniel requisivit de lege atque sententia ab Arioch principe militiæ regis, qui egressus fuerat ad interficiendos sapientes Babylonis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et interrogavit eum, qui a rege potestatem acceperat, quam ob causam tam crudelis sententia a facie regis esset egressa. Cum ergo rem indicasset Arioch Danieli,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Daniel ingressus rogavit regem ut tempus daret sibi ad solutionem indicandam regi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ingressus est domum suam, Ananiæque et Misaëli et Azariæ, sociis suis, indicavit negotium,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: ut quærerent misericordiam a facie Dei cæli super sacramento isto, et non perirent Daniel et socii ejus cum ceteris sapientibus Babylonis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc Danieli mysterium per visionem nocte revelatum est : et benedixit Daniel Deum cæli,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et locutus ait : Sit nomen Domini benedictum a sæculo et usque in sæculum : quia sapientia et fortitudo ejus sunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ipse mutat tempora, et ætates : transfert regna, atque constituit : dat sapientiam sapientibus, et scientiam intelligentibus disciplinam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ipse revelat profunda et abscondita, et novit in tenebris constituta : et lux cum eo est.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tibi, Deus patrum nostrorum, confiteor, teque laudo, quia sapientiam et fortitudinem dedisti mihi, et nunc ostendisti mihi quæ rogavimus te, quia sermonem regis aperuisti nobis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post hæc Daniel ingressus ad Arioch, quem constituerat rex ut perderet sapientes Babylonis, sic ei locutus est : Sapientes Babylonis ne perdas : introduc me in conspectu regis, et solutionem regi narrabo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc Arioch festinus introduxit Danielem ad regem, et dixit ei : Inveni hominem de filiis transmigrationis Juda, qui solutionem regi annuntiet.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondit rex, et dixit Danieli, cujus nomen erat Baltassar : Putasne vere potes mihi indicare somnium, quod vidi, et interpretationem ejus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et respondens Daniel coram rege, ait : Mysterium, quod rex interrogat, sapientes, magi, arioli, et aruspices nequeunt indicare regi :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sed est Deus in cælo revelans mysteria, qui indicavit tibi, rex Nabuchodonosor, quæ ventura sunt in novissimis temporibus. Somnium tuum, et visiones capitis tui in cubili tuo hujuscemodi sunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tu, rex, cogitare cœpisti in strato tuo, quid esset futurum post hæc : et qui revelat mysteria, ostendit tibi quæ ventura sunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Mihi quoque non in sapientia, quæ est in me plus quam in cunctis viventibus, sacramentum hoc revelatum est : sed ut interpretatio regi manifesta fieret, et cogitationes mentis tuæ scires.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tu, rex, videbas, et ecce quasi statua una grandis : statua illa magna, et statura sublimis stabat contra te, et intuitus ejus erat terribilis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hujus statuæ caput ex auro optimo erat, pectus autem et brachia de argento, porro venter et femora ex ære,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: tibiæ autem ferreæ : pedum quædam pars erat ferrea, quædam autem fictilis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Videbas ita, donec abscissus est lapis de monte sine manibus : et percussit statuam in pedibus ejus ferreis et fictilibus, et comminuit eos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc contrita sunt pariter ferrum, testa, æs, argentum, et aurum, et redacta quasi in favillam æstivæ areæ, quæ rapta sunt vento, nullusque locus inventus est eis : lapis autem, qui percusserat statuam, factus est mons magnus, et implevit universam terram.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hoc est somnium : interpretationem quoque ejus dicemus coram te, rex.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tu rex regum es : et Deus cæli regnum, et fortitudinem, et imperium, et gloriam dedit tibi :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et omnia, in quibus habitant filii hominum, et bestiæ agri : volucres quoque cæli dedit in manu tua, et sub ditione tua universa constituit : tu es ergo caput aureum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et post te consurget regnum aliud minus te argenteum : et regnum tertium aliud æreum, quod imperabit universæ terræ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et regnum quartum erit velut ferrum : quomodo ferrum comminuit, et domat omnia, sic comminuet, et conteret omnia hæc.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro quia vidisti pedum, et digitorum partem testæ figuli, et partem ferream, regnum divisum erit : quod tamen de plantario ferri orietur, secundum quod vidisti ferrum mistum testæ ex luto.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et digitos pedum ex parte ferreos, et ex parte fictiles : ex parte regnum erit solidum, et ex parte contritum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod autem vidisti ferrum mistum testæ ex luto, commiscebuntur quidem humano semine, sed non adhærebunt sibi, sicut ferrum misceri non potest testæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In diebus autem regnorum illorum suscitabit Deus cæli regnum, quod in æternum non dissipabitur, et regnum ejus alteri populo non tradetur : comminuet autem, et consumet universa regna hæc, et ipsum stabit in æternum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Secundum quod vidisti, quod de monte abscissus est lapis sine manibus, et comminuit testam, et ferrum, et æs, et argentum, et aurum, Deus magnus ostendit regi quæ ventura sunt postea : et verum est somnium, et fidelis interpretatio ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc rex Nabuchodonosor cecidit in faciem suam, et Danielem adoravit, et hostias, et incensum præcepit ut sacrificarent ei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Loquens ergo rex, ait Danieli : Vere Deus vester Deus deorum est, et Dominus regum, et revelans mysteria : quoniam tu potuisti aperire hoc sacramentum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc rex Danielem in sublime extulit, et munera multa et magna dedit ei : et constituit eum principem super omnes provincias Babylonis, et præfectum magistratuum super cunctos sapientes Babylonis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Daniel autem postulavit a rege, et constituit super opera provinciæ Babylonis Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago : ipse autem Daniel erat in foribus regis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nabuchodonosor rex fecit statuam auream, altitudine cubitorum sexaginta, latitudine cubitorum sex, et statuit eam in campo Dura, provinciæ Babylonis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Itaque Nabuchodonosor rex misit ad congregandos satrapas, magistratus, et judices, duces, et tyrannos, et præfectos, omnesque principes regionum, ut convenirent ad dedicationem statuæ quam erexerat Nabuchodonosor rex.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc congregati sunt satrapæ, magistratus, et judices, duces, et tyranni, et optimates, qui erant in potestatibus constituti, et universi principes regionum, ut convenirent ad dedicationem statuæ, quam erexerat Nabuchodonosor rex. Stabant autem in conspectu statuæ, quam posuerat Nabuchodonosor rex :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et præco clamabat valenter : Vobis dicitur populis, tribubus, et linguis :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: in hora qua audieritis sonitum tubæ, et fistulæ, et citharæ, sambucæ, et psalterii, et symphoniæ, et universi generis musicorum, cadentes adorate statuam auream, quam constituit Nabuchodonosor rex.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Si quis autem non prostratus adoraverit, eadem hora mittetur in fornacem ignis ardentis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post hæc igitur, statim ut audierunt omnes populi sonitum tubæ, fistulæ, et citharæ, sambucæ, et psalterii, et symphoniæ, et omnis generis musicorum, cadentes omnes populi, tribus, et linguæ adoraverunt statuam auream, quam constituerat Nabuchodonosor rex.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Statimque in ipso tempore accedentes viri Chaldæi accusaverunt Judæos :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: dixeruntque Nabuchodonosor regi : Rex, in æternum vive !

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: tu, rex, posuisti decretum, ut omnis homo, qui audierit sonitum tubæ, fistulæ, et citharæ, sambucæ, et psalterii, et symphoniæ, et universi generis musicorum, prosternat se, et adoret statuam auream :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: si quis autem non procidens adoraverit, mittatur in fornacem ignis ardentis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Sunt ergo viri Judæi, quos constituisti super opera regionis Babylonis, Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago : viri isti contempserunt, rex, decretum tuum : deos tuos non colunt, et statuam auream, quam erexisti, non adorant.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc Nabuchodonosor, in furore et in ira, præcepit ut adducerentur Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago : qui confestim adducti sunt in conspectu regis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Pronuntiansque Nabuchodonosor rex, ait eis : Verene Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago, deos meos non colitis, et statuam auream, quam constitui, non adoratis ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: nunc ergo si estis parati, quacumque hora audieritis sonitum tubæ, fistulæ, citharæ, sambucæ, et psalterii, et symphoniæ, omnisque generis musicorum, prosternite vos, et adorate statuam, quam feci : quod si non adoraveritis, eadem hora mittemini in fornacem ignis ardentis : et quis est Deus, qui eripiet vos de manu mea ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Respondentes Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago, dixerunt regi Nabuchodonosor : Non oportet nos de hac re respondere tibi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ecce enim Deus noster, quem colimus, potest eripere nos de camino ignis ardentis, et de manibus tuis, o rex, liberare.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod si noluerit, notum sit tibi, rex, quia deos tuos non colimus, et statuam auream, quam erexisti, non adoramus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc Nabuchodonosor repletus est furore, et aspectus faciei illius immutatus est super Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago : et præcepit ut succenderetur fornax septuplum quam succendi consueverat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et viris fortissimis de exercitu suo jussit ut ligatis pedibus Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago, mitterent eos in fornacem ignis ardentis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et confestim viri illi vincti, cum braccis suis, et tiaris, et calceamentis, et vestibus, missi sunt in medium fornacis ignis ardentis :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: nam jussio regis urgebat. Fornax autem succensa erat nimis : porro viros illos, qui miserant Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago, interfecit flamma ignis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Viri autem hi tres, id est, Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago, ceciderunt in medio camino ignis ardentis, colligati.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ambulabant in medio flammæ, laudantes Deum, et benedicentes Domino.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Stans autem Azarias oravit sic, aperiensque os suum in medio ignis, ait :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Benedictus es, Domine Deus patrum nostrorum, et laudabile, et gloriosum nomen tuum in sæcula :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quia justus es in omnibus, quæ fecisti nobis, et universa opera tua vera, et viæ tuæ rectæ, et omnia judicia tua vera.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Judicia enim vera fecisti juxta omnia, quæ induxisti super nos, et super civitatem sanctam patrum nostrorum Jerusalem : quia in veritate et in judicio induxisti omnia hæc propter peccata nostra.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Peccavimus enim, et inique egimus recedentes a te, et deliquimus in omnibus :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et præcepta tua non audivimus, nec observavimus, nec fecimus sicut præceperas nobis ut bene nobis esset.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Omnia ergo, quæ induxisti super nos, et universa quæ fecisti nobis, in vero judicio fecisti ;

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et tradidisti nos in manibus inimicorum nostrorum iniquorum, et pessimorum, prævaricatorumque, et regi injusto, et pessimo ultra omnem terram.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et nunc non possumus aperire os : confusio, et opprobrium facti sumus servis tuis, et his qui colunt te.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: In eadem hora apparuerunt digiti, quasi manus hominis scribentis contra candelabrum in superficie parietis aulæ regiæ : et rex aspiciebat articulos manus scribentis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc facies regis commutata est, et cogitationes ejus conturbabant eum : et compages renum ejus solvebantur, et genua ejus ad se invicem collidebantur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Exclamavit itaque rex fortiter ut introducerent magos, Chaldæos, et aruspices. Et proloquens rex ait sapientibus Babylonis : Quicumque legerit scripturam hanc, et interpretationem ejus manifestam mihi fecerit, purpura vestietur, et torquem auream habebit in collo, et tertius in regno meo erit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc ingressi omnes sapientes regis non potuerunt nec scripturam legere, nec interpretationem indicare regi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Unde rex Baltassar satis conturbatus est, et vultus illius immutatus est ; sed et optimates ejus turbabantur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Regina autem pro re, quæ acciderat regi et optimatibus ejus, domum convivii ingressa est : et proloquens ait : Rex, in æternum vive ! non te conturbent cogitationes tuæ, neque facies tua immutetur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Est vir in regno tuo, qui spiritum deorum sanctorum habet in se, et in diebus patris tui scientia et sapientia inventæ sunt in eo : nam et rex Nabuchodonosor pater tuus principem magorum, incantatorum, Chaldæorum, et aruspicum constituit eum, pater, inquam, tuus, o rex :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: quia spiritus amplior, et prudentia, intelligentiaque et interpretatio somniorum, et ostensio secretorum, ac solutio ligatorum inventæ sunt in eo, hoc est in Daniele : cui rex posuit nomen Baltassar. Nunc itaque Daniel vocetur, et interpretationem narrabit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Igitur introductus est Daniel coram rege : ad quem præfatus rex ait : Tu es Daniel de filiis captivitatis Judæ, quem adduxit pater meus rex de Judæa ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: audivi de te, quoniam spiritum deorum habeas, et scientia, intelligentiaque ac sapientia ampliores inventæ sunt in te.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et nunc introgressi sunt in conspectu meo sapientes magi, ut scripturam hanc legerent, et interpretationem ejus indicarent mihi : et nequiverunt sensum hujus sermonis edicere.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro ego audivi de te, quod possis obscura interpretari, et ligata dissolvere : si ergo vales scripturam legere, et interpretationem ejus indicare mihi, purpura vestieris, et torquem auream circa collum tuum habebis, et tertius in regno meo princeps eris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ad quæ respondens Daniel, ait coram rege : Munera tua sint tibi, et dona domus tuæ alteri da : scripturam autem legam tibi, rex, et interpretationem ejus ostendam tibi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: O rex, Deus altissimus regnum et magnificentiam, gloriam et honorem dedit Nabuchodonosor patri tuo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et propter magnificentiam, quam dederat ei, universi populi, tribus, et linguæ tremebant, et metuebant eum : quos volebat, interficiebat : et quos volebat, percutiebat : et quos volebat, exaltabat : et quos volebat, humiliabat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quando autem elevatum est cor ejus, et spiritus illius obfirmatus est ad superbiam, depositus est de solio regni sui, et gloria ejus ablata est :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et a filiis hominum ejectus est, sed et cor ejus cum bestiis positum est, et cum onagris erat habitatio ejus : fœnum quoque ut bos comedebat, et rore cæli corpus ejus infectum est, donec cognosceret quod potestatem haberet Altissimus in regno hominum, et quemcumque voluerit, suscitabit super illud.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tu quoque, filius ejus Baltassar, non humiliasti cor tuum, cum scires hæc omnia :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: sed adversum Dominatorem cæli elevatus es : et vasa domus ejus allata sunt coram te, et tu, et optimates tui, et uxores tuæ, et concubinæ tuæ vinum bibistis in eis : deos quoque argenteos, et aureos, et æreos, ferreos, ligneosque et lapideos, qui non vident, neque audiunt, neque sentiunt, laudasti : porro Deum, qui habet flatum tuum in manu sua, et omnes vias tuas, non glorificasti.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Idcirco ab eo missus est articulus manus, quæ scripsit hoc quod exaratum est.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæc est autem scriptura, quæ digesta est : Mane, Thecel, Phares.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et hæc est interpretatio sermonis. Mane : numeravit Deus regnum tuum, et complevit illud.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Thecel : appensus es in statera, et inventus es minus habens.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Phares : divisum est regnum tuum, et datum est Medis, et Persis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc, jubente rege, indutus est Daniel purpura, et circumdata est torques aurea collo ejus : et prædicatum est de eo quod haberet potestatem tertius in regno suo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Eadem nocte interfectus est Baltassar rex Chaldæus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et Darius Medus successit in regnum, annos natus sexaginta duos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Placuit Dario, et constituit super regnum satrapas centum viginti ut essent in toto regno suo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et super eos principes tres, ex quibus Daniel unus erat : ut satrapæ illis redderent rationem, et rex non sustineret molestiam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Igitur Daniel superabat omnes principes et satrapas, quia spiritus Dei amplior erat in illo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro rex cogitabat constituere eum super omne regnum : unde principes, et satrapæ quærebant occasionem ut invenirent Danieli ex latere regis : nullamque causam, et suspicionem reperire potuerunt, eo quod fidelis esset, et omnis culpa, et suspicio non inveniretur in eo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Dixerunt ergo viri illi : Non inveniemus Danieli huic aliquam occasionem, nisi forte in lege Dei sui.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc principes et satrapæ surripuerunt regi, et sic locuti sunt ei : Dari rex, in æternum vive !

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: consilium inierunt omnes principes regni tui, magistratus, et satrapæ, senatores, et judices, ut decretum imperatorium exeat, et edictum : ut omnis, qui petierit aliquam petitionem a quocumque deo et homine usque ad triginta dies, nisi a te, rex, mittatur in lacum leonum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Nunc itaque rex, confirma sententiam, et scribe decretum : ut non immutetur quod statutum est a Medis et Persis, nec prævaricari cuiquam liceat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro rex Darius proposuit edictum, et statuit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod cum Daniel comperisset, id est, constitutam legem, ingressus est domum suam : et fenestris apertis in cœnaculo suo contra Jerusalem tribus temporibus in die flectebat genua sua, et adorabat, confitebaturque coram Deo suo sicut et ante facere consueverat.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Viri ergo illi curiosius inquirentes invenerunt Danielem orantem, et obsecrantem Deum suum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et accedentes locuti sunt regi super edicto : Rex, numquid non constituisti ut omnis homo qui rogaret quemquam de diis et hominibus usque ad dies triginta, nisi te, rex, mitteretur in lacum leonum ? Ad quos respondens rex, ait : Verus est sermo juxta decretum Medorum atque Persarum, quod prævaricari non licet.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc respondentes dixerunt coram rege : Daniel de filiis captivitatis Juda, non curavit de lege tua, et de edicto quod constituisti : sed tribus temporibus per diem orat obsecratione sua.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Quod verbum cum audisset rex, satis contristatus est : et pro Daniele posuit cor ut liberaret eum, et usque ad occasum solis laborabat ut erueret illum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Viri autem illi, intelligentes regem, dixerunt ei : Scito, rex, quia lex Medorum atque Persarum est ut omne decretum, quod constituerit rex, non liceat immutari.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc rex præcepit, et adduxerunt Danielem, et miserunt eum in lacum leonum. Dixitque rex Danieli : Deus tuus, quem colis semper, ipse liberabit te.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Allatusque est lapis unus, et positus est super os laci : quem obsignavit rex annulo suo, et annulo optimatum suorum, ne quid fieret contra Danielem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et abiit rex in domum suam, et dormivit incœnatus, cibique non sunt allati coram eo, insuper et somnus recessit ab eo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc rex primo diluculo consurgens, festinus ad lacum leonum perrexit :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: appropinquansque lacui, Danielem voce lacrimabili inclamavit, et affatus est eum : Daniel serve Dei viventis, Deus tuus, cui tu servis semper, putasne valuit te liberare a leonibus ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et Daniel regi respondens ait : Rex, in æternum vive !

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Deus meus misit angelum suum, et conclusit ora leonum, et non nocuerunt mihi : quia coram eo justitia inventa est in me : sed et coram te, rex, delictum non feci.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc vehementer rex gavisus est super eo, et Danielem præcepit educi de lacu : eductusque est Daniel de lacu, et nulla læsio inventa est in eo, quia credidit Deo suo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Jubente autem rege, adducti sunt viri illi, qui accusaverant Danielem : et in lacum leonum missi sunt, ipsi, et filii, et uxores eorum : et non pervenerunt usque ad pavimentum laci, donec arriperent eos leones, et omnia ossa eorum comminuerunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc Darius rex scripsit universis populis, tribubus, et linguis habitantibus in universa terra : Pax vobis multiplicetur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: A me constitutum est decretum, ut in universo imperio et regno meo, tremiscant et paveant Deum Danielis : ipse est enim Deus vivens, et æternus in sæcula, et regnum ejus non dissipabitur, et potestas ejus usque in æternum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Ipse liberator atque salvator, faciens signa et mirabilia in cælo et in terra : qui liberavit Danielem de lacu leonum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro Daniel perseveravit usque ad regnum Darii, regnumque Cyri Persæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Anno primo Baltassar regis Babylonis, Daniel somnium vidit : visio autem capitis ejus in cubili suo : et somnium scribens, brevi sermone comprehendit : summatimque perstringens, ait :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Videbam in visione mea nocte : et ecce quatuor venti cæli pugnabant in mari magno.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et quatuor bestiæ grandes ascendebant de mari diversæ inter se.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Prima quasi leæna, et alas habebat aquilæ : aspiciebam donec evulsæ sunt alæ ejus, et sublata est de terra, et super pedes quasi homo stetit ; et cor hominis datum est ei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ecce bestia alia similis urso in parte stetit : et tres ordines erant in ore ejus, et in dentibus ejus, et sic dicebant ei : Surge, comede carnes plurimas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post hæc aspiciebam, et ecce alia quasi pardus, et alas habebat quasi avis, quatuor super se : et quatuor capita erant in bestia, et potestas data est ei.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post hæc aspiciebam in visione noctis, et ecce bestia quarta terribilis atque mirabilis, et fortis nimis : dentes ferreos habebat magnos, comedens atque comminuens, et reliqua pedibus suis conculcans : dissimilis autem erat ceteris bestiis quas videram ante eam, et habebat cornua decem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Considerabam cornua, et ecce cornu aliud parvulum ortum est de medio eorum : et tria de cornibus primis evulsa sunt a facie ejus : et ecce oculi, quasi oculi hominis erant in cornu isto, et os loquens ingentia.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Aspiciebam donec throni positi sunt, et antiquus dierum sedit. Vestimentum ejus candidum quasi nix, et capilli capitis ejus quasi lana munda : thronus ejus flammæ ignis : rotæ ejus ignis accensus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Fluvius igneus rapidusque egrediebatur a facie ejus. Millia millium ministrabant ei, et decies millies centena millia assistebant ei : judicium sedit, et libri aperti sunt.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Aspiciebam propter vocem sermonum grandium, quos cornu illud loquebatur : et vidi quoniam interfecta esset bestia, et perisset corpus ejus, et traditum esset ad comburendum igni :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: aliarum quoque bestiarum ablata esset potestas, et tempora vitæ constituta essent eis usque ad tempus et tempus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Aspiciebam ergo in visione noctis, et ecce cum nubibus cæli quasi filius hominis veniebat, et usque ad antiquum dierum pervenit : et in conspectu ejus obtulerunt eum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dedit ei potestatem, et honorem, et regnum : et omnes populi, tribus, et linguæ ipsi servient : potestas ejus, potestas æterna, quæ non auferetur : et regnum ejus, quod non corrumpetur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Horruit spiritus meus : ego Daniel territus sum in his, et visiones capitis mei conturbaverunt me.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Accessi ad unum de assistentibus, et veritatem quærebam ab eo de omnibus his. Qui dixit mihi interpretationem sermonum, et docuit me :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hæ quatuor bestiæ magnæ, quatuor sunt regna, quæ consurgent de terra.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Suscipient autem regnum sancti Dei altissimi, et obtinebunt regnum usque in sæculum, et sæculum sæculorum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Post hoc volui diligenter discere de bestia quarta, quæ erat dissimilis valde ab omnibus, et terribilis nimis : dentes et ungues ejus ferrei : comedebat, et comminuebat, et reliqua pedibus suis conculcabat :

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: et de cornibus decem, quæ habebat in capite, et de alio, quod ortum fuerat, ante quod ceciderant tria cornua : et de cornu illo, quod habebat oculos, et os loquens grandia, et majus erat ceteris.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Aspiciebam, et ecce cornu illud faciebat bellum adversus sanctos, et prævalebat eis,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: donec venit antiquus dierum, et judicium dedit sanctis Excelsi, et tempus advenit, et regnum obtinuerunt sancti.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et sic ait : Bestia quarta, regnum quartum erit in terra, quod majus erit omnibus regnis, et devorabit universam terram, et conculcabit, et comminuet eam.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro cornua decem ipsius regni, decem reges erunt : et alius consurget post eos, et ipse potentior erit prioribus, et tres reges humiliabit.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et sermones contra Excelsum loquetur, et sanctos Altissimi conteret : et putabit quod possit mutare tempora, et leges : et tradentur in manu ejus usque ad tempus, et tempora, et dimidium temporis.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et judicium sedebit, ut auferatur potentia, et conteratur, et dispereat usque in finem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Regnum autem, et potestas, et magnitudo regni, quæ est subter omne cælum, detur populo sanctorum Altissimi : cujus regnum, regnum sempiternum est, et omnes reges servient ei, et obedient.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hucusque finis verbi. Ego Daniel multum cogitationibus meis conturbabar, et facies mea mutata est in me : verbum autem in corde meo conservavi.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Anno tertio regni Baltassar regis, visio apparuit mihi. Ego Daniel, post id quod videram in principio,

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: vidi in visione mea, cum essem in Susis castro, quod est in Ælam regione : vidi autem in visione esse me super portam Ulai.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et levavi oculus meos, et vidi : et ecce aries unus stabat ante paludem, habens cornua excelsa, et unum excelsius altero atque succrescens. Postea

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: vidi arietem cornibus ventilantem contra occidentem, et contra aquilonem, et contra meridiem, et omnes bestiæ non poterant resistere ei, neque liberari de manu ejus : fecitque secundum voluntatem suam, et magnificatus est.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ego intelligebam : ecce autem hircus caprarum veniebat ab occidente super faciem totius terræ, et non tangebat terram : porro hircus habebat cornu insigne inter oculos suos.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et venit usque ad arietem illum cornutum, quem videram stantem ante portam, et cucurrit ad eum in impetu fortitudinis suæ.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Cumque appropinquasset prope arietem, efferatus est in eum, et percussit arietem : et comminuit duo cornua ejus, et non poterat aries resistere ei : cumque eum misisset in terram, conculcavit, et nemo quibat liberare arietem de manu ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Hircus autem caprarum magnus factus est nimis : cumque crevisset, fractum est cornu magnum, et orta sunt quatuor cornua subter illud per quatuor ventos cæli.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: De uno autem ex eis egressum est cornu unum modicum : et factum est grande contra meridiem, et contra orientem, et contra fortitudinem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et magnificatum est usque ad fortitudinem cæli : et dejecit de fortitudine, et de stellis, et conculcavit eas.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et usque ad principem fortitudinis magnificatum est : et ab eo tulit juge sacrificium, et dejecit locum sanctificationis ejus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Robur autem datum est ei contra juge sacrificium propter peccata : et prosternetur veritas in terra, et faciet, et prosperabitur.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et audivi unum de sanctis loquentem : et dixit unus sanctus alteri nescio cui loquenti : Usquequo visio, et juge sacrificium, et peccatum desolationis quæ facta est : et sanctuarium, et fortitudo conculcabitur ?

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et dixit ei : Usque ad vesperam et mane, dies duo millia trecenti : et mundabitur sanctuarium.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Factum est autem cum viderem ego Daniel visionem, et quærerem intelligentiam : ecce stetit in conspectu meo quasi species viri.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et audivi vocem viri inter Ulai : et clamavit, et ait : Gabriel, fac intelligere istam visionem.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et apprehendit eum angelus Domini in vertice ejus, et portavit eum capillo capitis sui, posuitque eum in Babylone supra lacum in impetu spiritus sui.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et clamavit Habacuc, dicens : Daniel serve Dei, tolle prandium quod misit tibi Deus.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et ait Daniel : Recordatus es mei, Deus, et non dereliquisti diligentes te.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Surgensque Daniel comedit. Porro angelus Domini restituit Habacuc confestim in loco suo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Venit ergo rex die septimo ut lugeret Danielem : et venit ad lacum, et introspexit, et ecce Daniel sedens in medio leonum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Et exclamavit voce magna rex, dicens : Magnus es, Domine Deus Danielis. Et extraxit eum de lacu leonum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Porro illos, qui perditionis ejus causa fuerant, intromisit in lacum, et devorati sunt in momento coram eo.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)

text: Tunc rex ait : Paveant omnes habitantes in universa terra Deum Danielis : quia ipse est salvator, faciens signa et mirabilia in terra : qui liberavit Danielem de lacu leonum.

source.electronic_edition: The Clementine Vulgate Project (ed. Michael Tweedale, London, 2005)
